worktrees.name: session-142239--the-first-page-of-the-github-d
worktrees.task: The first page of the github documents for this project is way too large.   and has multiple errors.   First, the problem that we are solving is not just context rot and scoping,   There is the problem of human escalation -- too much and the human starts rubber stamping, too little and the intent diverges.   3) importance of backtracking - when errors are discovered you need to go back sometimes to the very beginning.   This is not captured well in the plan-execute model 4) intent gap - human's arent partners in the conversation.   The agent builds what it wants to build with little understanding of what was requested.

worktrees.name: editorial-60cd10ad--editorial-review-of-a-6-c
worktrees.task:
Editorial review of a 6-chapter popular-history manuscript on the history of knock-knock jokes.

DELIVERABLE: Write a file to disk at this exact absolute path using the Write tool:
/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/editorial-notes.md

FILES TO READ (read all of these before writing your review):
- Intent: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/INTENT.md
- Spec: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/SPEC.md
- Research dossier: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/research-dossier.md
- Chapter 1: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/chapter-1-origins-and-precursors.md
- Chapter 2: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/chapter-2-the-1930s-craze.md
- Chapter 3: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/chapter-3-golden-age-and-backlash.md
- Chapter 4: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/chapter-4-popular-culture.md
- Chapter 5: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/chapter-5-global-and-internet.md
- Chapter 6: /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/joke-book/.worktrees/session-183024--i-would-like-to-create-a-5-7-c/book/chapter-6-why-it-works.md

REVIEW CRITERIA (evaluate ALL of these):
1. Prose register: flag any passages that read like encyclopedia entries, listicles, or Wikipedia summaries rather than narrative nonfiction
2. Citation integrity: every named source should trace to the research dossier; flag any claim that names a date, person, or publication without a dossier source
3. Speculation labeling: every speculative claim must be labeled as speculation; flag unlabeled speculation
4. Source gap honesty: paywalled sources (Harrisburg Telegraph, News Herald, Oakland Tribune, Bett 1929, McGhee 1979, Raskin 1985, etc.) must be acknowledged as unaccessed; flag any passage that asserts content from these sources as direct fact
5. Narrative through-lines: each chapter should have a clear organizing question or tension — flag chapters that feel like chronological fact-lists rather than narratives
6. Humor and voice: the prose should have personality and wit appropriate to the subject matter — flag passages that feel dry, mechanical, or over-formal
7. Cross-chapter consistency: tone, register, and quality should be consistent across all six chapters — note any that feel like outliers
8. Chapter warrant: each chapter should earn its place — note if any chapter feels redundant or could be merged without loss

OUTPUT FORMAT for editorial-notes.md:
- A brief overall assessment (2-3 paragraphs)
- Chapter-by-chapter notes (one section per chapter, with specific line references or quoted passages where issues are identified)
- Cross-chapter notes (issues spanning multiple chapters)
- A prioritized list of revisions: HIGH (must fix before the manuscript is done), MEDIUM (should fix if possible), LOW (optional improvements)

The review is complete when the notes file is written to disk at the output path and every success criterion in INTENT.md has been evaluated.

worktrees.name: session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-

worktrees.task:
Read every design document listed below and produce a structured inventory written as a Markdown file. All work is read-only except for writing the final inventory file. No code changes.

## Working directory / worktree root
`/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/`

## Documents to read (absolute paths)
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/poc-architecture.md`
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/intent-engineering-spec.md`
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/intent-engineering-detailed-design.md`
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/learning-system.md`
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/PROXY_WIRE_TASK.md`
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/GLOSSARY.md`
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/POC.md`
- `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/projects/POC/docs/agentic-cfa-spec.docx` — ATTEMPT TO READ THIS. Report whether it is machine-readable.

## Output file to write
`/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/doc_inventory.md`

## What the inventory must contain

For each document, produce a section with these subsections:
1. **Named features** — every named feature, component, or system the doc describes (e.g., "CfA state machine", "approval gate", "proxy agent", "learning pipeline")
2. **States/transitions** — any state machine states, lifecycle phases, or transition sequences described
3. **Behaviors** — specific behaviors the system is supposed to exhibit (e.g., "on INTENT_ASSERT, proxy auto-approves if confidence > threshold")
4. **Data structures** — any named data models, schemas, or file formats described
5. **Marked futures** — anything explicitly marked as future / not yet implemented in the doc itself

Format each item as a bullet with enough detail that a developer could search for the corresponding implementation.

Also include at the TOP of the inventory:
- **GLOSSARY TERMS** section — the complete list of terms from GLOSSARY.md with their one-line definitions

Also include:
- **docx accessibility** section — whether `agentic-cfa-spec.docx` was readable; if readable, summarize its unique content (not covered by the .md docs)

Be thorough. This inventory is the reference baseline for a gap analysis — missing items here become missing gaps. Every named concept, state, behavior, and data structure matters.

`/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/code_inventory.md`

## Files to read

All orchestrator modules (read them all):
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/engine.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/session.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/actors.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/claude_runner.py`

- `projects/POC/orchestrator/learnings.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/procedural_learning.py`

- `projects/POC/orchestrator/approval_gate.py` (check if it exists first)
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/events.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/phase_config.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/skill_lookup.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/state_writer.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/worktree.py`
- `projects/POC/orchestrator/merge.py`

- Any other `.py` files in `projects/POC/scripts/` that are imported by the orchestrator modules above

## What to produce in code_inventory.md

### Section 1: Module summary table
A table: module name | purpose (one sentence) | key classes/functions | what design concept it implements

### Section 2: Per-module detail
For each module, list:
- All public classes with their fields/methods (brief)
- All public functions with signature and one-line purpose
- Side effects (file writes, subprocess calls, network calls) — where they live
- Import dependencies (what this module imports from other orchestrator modules)

### Section 3: GAPS.md spot-check
Read `projects/POC/orchestrator/GAPS.md`. Find all items marked `[RESOLVED]` (or `[PASS]`). Pick 10 of them that represent a mix of different features. For each, check the actual current Python source to verify whether the behavior is actually implemented as described.

Report: item description | what GAPS.md claims | what the current code actually does | verdict: CONFIRMED / FALSE_POSITIVE / PARTIALLY_IMPLEMENTED

### Section 4: Import graph
For every orchestrator module, list what it imports from other orchestrator modules and from `projects/POC/scripts/`. Do not include stdlib or third-party imports — only intra-project dependencies.

### Section 5: Entrypoints
Identify the top-level entrypoints (files that can be invoked directly or imported by external callers). For each, trace the call graph one level deep.

Be thorough and precise. Line numbers are valuable when citing specific behaviors or patterns.

All file reads must use absolute paths under `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/`. Write the final output to `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-/code_inventory.md`.

worktrees.name: coding-5e52b215--task-orchestrator-source-
worktrees.task:
## Task: Orchestrator Source Inventory + GAPS.md Spot-Check (Phase 1)

Read every Python file in `projects/POC/orchestrator/` and the referenced scripts file. Produce a thorough internal inventory that will feed into a gap analysis and style audit.

### Files to read:
1. All `.py` files in `projects/POC/orchestrator/` — read each one fully
2. `projects/POC/scripts/cfa_state.py` — referenced by engine.py
3. `projects/POC/orchestrator/GAPS.md` — the existing gap tracking file (69 items, dated 2026-03-11)

### What to extract per Python module:
- Classes defined: names, purpose, key methods
- Functions defined: names, signatures, what they do
- Notable patterns: async usage, state management, external calls, file I/O
- Imports: what each module imports from (for dependency mapping)
- Anything that looks incomplete, stubbed, or marked TODO/FIXME

### RESOLVE Q2 — GAPS.md Spot-Check (critical):
Select 8–10 items from `orchestrator/GAPS.md` that are marked `[RESOLVED]`. For each one, verify against the current Python source whether the resolution is accurate. Record:
- Item number and description
- Status: CONFIRMED (actually resolved), CONTRADICTED (marked resolved but not), or PARTIAL (partially resolved)

If 3 or more `[RESOLVED]` items are NOT actually implemented, flag this explicitly as: "UNRELIABLE BASELINE — 3+ false resolutions found."

### Output:
Write a comprehensive inventory file to the worktree root: `code_inventory.md`

The worktree root is: `/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-092347--make-a-detailed-review-of-the-`

The file must include:
- One section per Python module
- Per-module: classes, functions, patterns, imports summary
- A section titled "GAPS.md Spot-Check Results" with all 8–10 checked items and their status
